events.text:
>
{"decision": "fail", "risk": "high", "work_stage": "rough-complete", "component_conditions": ["non-compliant", "installed-defective"], "percent_complete": 75, "value": 0, "sound_source": "", "confidence": 0.95, "event_time": 0, "rate": 0, "order": [], "workable": [], "findings": ["Slings are routed over unprotected edges, creating a cutting/abrasion hazard and reducing sling capacity.", "Edge protection is absent where slings contact structural edges or corners."], "actions": ["Immediately stop the lift and remove load if slings are under tension.", "Install rated edge protectors, saddles, or padding at all contact points between slings and edges.", "Inspect slings for damage; replace if cuts, fraying, or core damage are present.", "Re-rig using protective devices and have a competent person re-inspect before use."], "rationale": "ASME B30 and OSHA 1926.251 require slings to be protected from sharp edges and corners that could damage the sling. Running slings over unprotected edges is a recognized rigging violation that can cause sling failure and dropped loads.", "references": ["ASME B30.9 (Slings) - protection from sharp edges/corners", "ASME B30.10 - rigging practices", "OSHA 1926.251(a)(3) - protection from sharp edges", "OSHA 1926.251(a)(4) - safe rigging practices"]}
